text:
On the Occasion of the 50th Anniversary of the Mizar Project We describe an advanced Artificial Intelligence/Theorem Proving system that can automatically prove about 60% of the statements of the Mizar Mathematical Library (MML) in a “hammer” (large-theory) setting. For about 25% of the statements in MML this system can prove them using the special introduction and elimination theorems that are provided in the original human proofs. We achieve a much better performance in this mode, reaching a percentage of proved statements higher than 75%, which is much better than the Mizar40 benchmarks that we had 10 years ago.

This paper explains the methods and experiments we carried out in order to achieve the results presented. In particular it describes the E and Vampire prover implementations, as well as the machine-learning frameworks ENIGMA and Deepire that enhanced their performance, the learning-based premise selection strategies that were developed and tested, as well as the "prove-learn" loop incremental procedure for generating a corpus of millions of proofs to train our models. Finally, it demonstrates the robustness of our anonymous, logic-aware learning methods by being able to successfully transfer our most powerful models to the next version of the MML which includes terms that are not present in the training set. Finally, we conclude by mentioning a few particularly difficult theorems in Mizar that were proved fully automatically.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
